---
title: "Cases and Deaths by Health Zone"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
    self-contained: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    geometry: margin=2cm
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)

data_dir <- "/rds/general/user/acp25/home/MIMIC/Clean_data/Proj_2/CHLAA/analysis/data"

# ---- Load IDSR_dataset ----
combined <- read.csv(file.path(data_dir, "IDSR_dataset.csv"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
    mutate(date = as.Date(date))

# ---- Load admin_names for Province + Territory lookup ----
admin <- read.csv(file.path(data_dir, "admin_names.csv"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Build a lookup: hz -> Province, Territory
hz_lookup <- admin %>%
    select(adm1_province, adm2_territory, adm3_hz) %>%
    distinct(adm3_hz, .keep_all = TRUE)

# ---- Target HZs ----
target_hzs <- c(
    "goma", "kirotshe", "kindu", "alunguli", "opala",
    "bumbu", "kokolo", "ngiri_ngiri", "kingabwa", "limete",
    "maluku_i", "kiyambi", "kalemie", "nyemba", "kabalo",
    "kongolo", "nyiragongo"
)

# ---- Helper: build a nice title from admin_names ----
get_title <- function(hz_name) {
    row <- hz_lookup %>% filter(adm3_hz == hz_name)
    if (nrow(row) == 0) {
        # Fallback: use the hz name only (no province in IDSR_dataset)
        hz_label <- tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", hz_name))
        return(hz_label)
    }
    hz_label <- tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", hz_name))
    province <- row$adm1_province[1]
    paste0(hz_label, " — ", province)
}

# ---- Helper: make the overlaid barplot ----
plot_cases_deaths <- function(hz_name) {
    df <- combined %>%
        filter(hz == hz_name) %>%
        select(date, cases, deaths) %>%
        filter(!is.na(date))

    if (nrow(df) == 0) {
        return(ggplot() +
            annotate("text",
                x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
                label = paste("No data for", hz_name), size = 5
            ) +
            theme_void())
    }

    title_text <- get_title(hz_name)

    # Pivot to long for easier legend handling
    df_long <- df %>%
        pivot_longer(
            cols = c(cases, deaths),
            names_to = "measure",
            values_to = "count"
        ) %>%
        mutate(measure = factor(measure,
            levels = c("cases", "deaths"),
            labels = c("Cases", "Deaths")
        ))

    ggplot(df_long, aes(x = date, y = count, fill = measure)) +
        geom_col(position = "identity", width = 7, alpha = 0.7) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = c("Cases" = "steelblue", "Deaths" = "firebrick")) +
        labs(
            title = title_text,
            x = NULL,
            y = "Weekly count",
            fill = NULL
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
        theme(legend.position = "top")
}
```

```{r}
#| results: asis
for (hz_name in target_hzs) {
    hz_label <- tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", hz_name))
    cat("\n\n## ", hz_label, "\n\n", sep = "")
    p <- plot_cases_deaths(hz_name)
    print(p)
    cat("\n\n")
}
```

# Outbreak period (from hz_parameters.csv)

The plots below show cases and deaths restricted to each health zone's
own date window — from the earliest to the latest date mentioned for
that HZ in `hz_parameters.csv`.

```{r outbreak-period-setup}
# ---- Load hz_parameters.csv and derive per-HZ date windows ----
hz_params_long <- read.csv(file.path(data_dir, "hz_parameters.csv"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Build a lookup: for each HZ, find the min and max date across all its parameters
hz_date_windows <- hz_params_long %>%
    mutate(date_val = as.Date(value, format = "%Y-%m-%d")) %>%
    filter(!is.na(date_val)) %>%
    group_by(hz) %>%
    summarise(
        hz_start = min(date_val),
        hz_end   = max(date_val),
        .groups  = "drop"
    )

# ---- Helper: outbreak-period barplot with per-HZ date window ----
plot_cases_deaths_outbreak <- function(hz_name) {
    window <- hz_date_windows %>% filter(hz == hz_name)

    if (nrow(window) == 0) {
        return(ggplot() +
            annotate("text",
                x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
                label = paste("No date window for", hz_name),
                size = 5
            ) +
            theme_void())
    }

    hz_start <- window$hz_start
    hz_end <- window$hz_end

    # Show 3 months before and after the outbreak dates
    plot_start <- hz_start - 90
    plot_end <- hz_end + 90

    df <- combined %>%
        filter(
            hz == hz_name,
            date >= plot_start,
            date <= plot_end
        ) %>%
        select(date, cases, deaths) %>%
        filter(!is.na(date))

    if (nrow(df) == 0) {
        return(ggplot() +
            annotate("text",
                x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
                label = paste(
                    "No data for", hz_name,
                    "in outbreak period"
                ),
                size = 5
            ) +
            theme_void())
    }

    title_text <- get_title(hz_name)

    df_long <- df %>%
        pivot_longer(
            cols = c(cases, deaths),
            names_to = "measure",
            values_to = "count"
        ) %>%
        mutate(measure = factor(measure,
            levels = c("cases", "deaths"),
            labels = c("Cases", "Deaths")
        ))

    ggplot(df_long, aes(x = date, y = count, fill = measure)) +
        geom_col(position = "identity", width = 7, alpha = 0.7) +
        geom_vline(
            xintercept = c(hz_start, hz_end),
            linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5
        ) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = c(
            "Cases" = "steelblue",
            "Deaths" = "firebrick"
        )) +
        scale_x_date(
            date_breaks = "1 month", date_labels = "%Y-%m",
            limits = c(plot_start, plot_end)
        ) +
        labs(
            title = title_text,
            subtitle = paste0(
                "Outbreak period: ",
                format(hz_start, "%b %Y"), " – ",
                format(hz_end, "%b %Y"),
                "  (dashed lines = outbreak window)"
            ),
            x = NULL,
            y = "Weekly count",
            fill = NULL
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
        theme(
            legend.position = "top",
            axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
        )
}
```

```{r}
#| results: asis
for (hz_name in target_hzs) {
    hz_label <- tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", hz_name))
    cat("\n\n## ", hz_label, "\n\n", sep = "")
    p <- plot_cases_deaths_outbreak(hz_name)
    print(p)
    cat("\n\n")
}
```
